Title: Divergent evolution of extreme production of variant plant monounsaturated fatty acids
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Seed-specific coexpression in transgenic camelina; in vitro thioesterase assay with 18:1Δ6-ACP and 18:1Δ9-ACP; metabolite profiling by GC-FID/GC-MS
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Coexpression of TlFatA with TlDES6 in camelina increased petroselinic acid and its elongation products about three-fold relative to TlDES6 alone. 2. Top coexpression lines accumulated ~12% petroselinic acid and ~18% total Δ6-derived monoenes. 3. In vitro, TlFatA was ~10-fold more active with oleoyl-ACP than petroselinoyl-ACP, indicating enhancement in vivo is due to increased total thioesterase activity rather than strict petroselinoyl specificity.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes release of petroselinic acid from ACP and increases petroselinic-acid-derived fatty acid accumulation in engineered seeds
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potential precursor for lauric acid and adipic acid; low-caloric oil applications; solid-fat properties for margarine without hydrogenation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Seed-specific transgene expression in camelina seeds
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful gene-stacking component with TlDES6 and TlLPAT2 plus FAE1 suppression to raise petroselinic acid in oilseeds
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not specialized for petroselinoyl-ACP in vitro; bottleneck relief comes from elevated overall FatA activity

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Seed-specific coexpression with TlDES6 and TlFatA plus camelina FAE1 RNAi; seed fatty acid profiling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TlLPAT2 coexpression with TlDES6 and TlFatA in an FAE1-RNAi background increased petroselinic acid accumulation in camelina seeds. 2. This stack achieved up to ~20% petroselinic acid in T2 seeds, the highest level reported in engineered oilseed according to the paper. 3. TlLPAT2 is proposed to enhance incorporation of petroselinic acid into the sn-2 position of TAG.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances incorporation/storage of petroselinic acid into TAG and boosts seed petroselinic acid content
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potential industrial and nutritional oil trait
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Seed-specific transgene expression in camelina seeds
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important stacking gene for maximizing petroselinic acid deposition into storage oil
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Presented as a specialized downstream enzyme for TAG production using petroselinoyl-CoA substrates

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Seed-specific RNAi suppression combined with transgenic desaturase stacks; seed fatty acid profiling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RNAi suppression of camelina FAE1 reduced elongation of Δ6-derived 18-carbon monoenes to C20/C22 products. 2. In the T. alata desaturase background, FAE1 RNAi greatly reduced C20 elongation product and abolished detectable 22:1Δ12. 3. In the TlDES6+TlFatA+TlLPAT2 stack, FAE1 suppression helped raise petroselinic acid to ~20% by minimizing further elongation.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces elongation of unusual monoenes, thereby favoring accumulation of petroselinic acid or 18:1Δ8 over longer-chain products
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Seed-expressed endogenous gene targeted by RNAi
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key host-gene target for editing or RNAi to prevent unwanted elongation of engineered unusual fatty acids
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as host metabolic engineering target rather than donor transgene
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could reduce endogenous very-long-chain fatty acids in seed oil

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Thunbergia alata; Thunbergia laurifolia; Camelina sativa
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression comparison in developing seeds; metabolite interpretation from transgenic camelina and native seed fatty acid profiles
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KASII expression was ~3-fold lower in developing T. alata seeds than in T. laurifolia seeds. 2. Lower KASII expression is consistent with reduced elongation of 16:1Δ6-ACP in T. alata and consequent accumulation of sapienic acid. 3. Higher effective KASII capacity in camelina explains near-complete elongation of 16:1Δ6-derived intermediates to 18:1Δ8.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Controls plastid elongation flux of 16:1Δ6-ACP to 18:1Δ8-ACP, shaping sapienic versus elongated monoene accumulation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Developing seeds; lower in T. alata than T. laurifolia
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Measured in developing camelina seeds for ratio comparison
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Host target for suppressing elongation of 16:1Δ6 if sapienic acid accumulation is desired
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence is correlative expression analysis rather than direct knockout/overexpression
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May alter overall plastid fatty acid elongation balance

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Thunbergia laurifolia; Camelina sativa; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Fatty acid biosynthesis / unusual monounsaturated fatty acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a cis Δ6 double bond into stearoyl-ACP to form petroselinoyl-ACP
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms immediate activated precursor of petroselinic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cDNA cloning; recombinant enzyme assays with 16:0-ACP and 18:0-ACP; TLC/GC-MS product identification; crystal structure; transgenic camelina expression; E. coli expression
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DES6-1 is ~20-fold more active with 18:0-ACP than 16:0-ACP and produces a Δ6 monounsaturated product from 18:0-ACP. 2. Expression in camelina seeds produced petroselinic acid and elongation products. 3. Coexpression with FatA and TlLPAT2 plus FAE1 RNAi enabled petroselinic acid accumulation up to ~20% in engineered seeds. 4. Structural and mutational analyses established amino acid determinants of regiospecificity and functional divergence from Δ9 desaturases.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes petroselinic acid biosynthesis and is the major determinant of extreme petroselinic acid production
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potential precursor for lauric acid and adipic acid; low-caloric and solid-fat oil applications
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Seed-specific transgene expression in camelina; heterologous expression in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Primary donor enzyme for engineering petroselinic-acid-rich oils in plants and microbes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: DES6-2 is nearly identical paralog; crystal structure solved for TlDES6-1-related protein; mutants generated Δ9 and novel Δ10 regiospecificity
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing seeds; in vitro enzyme assay at 25°C
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Without pathway balancing, much product is elongated to C20/C22 monoenes

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Fatty acid biosynthesis / unusual monounsaturated fatty acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Predicted to introduce a cis Δ6 double bond into stearoyl-ACP
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms immediate precursor of petroselinic acid
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DES6-2 is a second T. laurifolia acyl-ACP desaturase cDNA sharing 98% amino acid identity with DES6-1. 2. It was identified among core candidate enzymes associated with petroselinic acid biosynthesis in seeds. 3. Functional assays in the Results focused primarily on DES6-1, so DES6-2 remains less directly validated.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely contributes to petroselinic acid biosynthesis, but direct functional evidence in this paper is limited
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because isolated from developing seeds as nearly identical paralog, but not the main experimentally characterized desaturase

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Fatty acid biosynthesis / unusual monounsaturated fatty acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a cis Δ6 double bond into palmitoyl-ACP to form sapienoyl-ACP
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms immediate precursor of sapienic acid and its elongation products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Purified recombinant enzyme assay; transgenic camelina expression; GC-MS product identification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The T. alata enzyme is ~6-fold more active with 16:0-ACP than 18:0-ACP. 2. It functions exclusively as a Δ6 desaturase with 16:0-ACP but shows mixed Δ6/Δ9 regiospecificity with 18:0-ACP. 3. Expression in camelina produced little 16:1Δ6 but substantial elongation products 18:1Δ8, 20:1Δ10, and 22:1Δ12, together ≥19% of total seed fatty acids.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes sapienic acid pathway entry; in camelina mainly drives accumulation of elongated Δ6-derived monoenes
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering Δ6 monoene oils, though host elongation capacity strongly redirects products
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contrasts with TlDES6 by substrate preference and resulting product spectrum
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing seeds; in vitro enzyme assay at 25°C
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Product diverted strongly into elongated species in camelina

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a cis Δ9 double bond into stearoyl-ACP to form oleoyl-ACP
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A more distantly related T. laurifolia Δ9-18:0-ACP desaturase was identified from seed transcriptome sequences. 2. It provides evolutionary context for recent divergence of TlDES6 enzymes from canonical Δ9 desaturases. 3. It was not the main target of functional assays in the Results.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represents canonical competing desaturation activity rather than the unusual-fatty-acid trait determinant
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful comparator for enzyme engineering of regiospecificity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contextual evolutionary comparator, not principal causal engineering gene
